schema.name: data
schema.description: Incident data for a create request.

children.name: attributes
children.description: The incident's attributes for a create request.

children.name: customer_impact_scope
children.description: Required if `customer_impacted:"true"`. A summary of the impact customers experienced during the incident.

children.name: customer_impacted
children.description: A flag indicating whether the incident caused customer impact.

children.name: fields
children.description: A condensed view of the user-defined fields for which to create initial selections.

children.name: incident_type_uuid
children.description: A unique identifier that represents an incident type. The default incident type will be used if this property is not provided.

children.name: initial_cells
children.description: An array of initial timeline cells to be placed at the beginning of the incident timeline.
The timeline cell's attributes for a create request.

fields.name: cell_type
fields.description: Type of the Markdown timeline cell.

children.name: content
children.description: The Markdown content of the cell.

fields.name: important
fields.description: A flag indicating whether the timeline cell is important and should be highlighted.

children.name: is_test
children.description: A flag indicating whether the incident is a test incident.

children.name: notification_handles
children.description: Notification handles that will be notified of the incident at creation.

children.name: display_name
children.description: The name of the notified handle.

children.name: handle
children.description: The handle used for the notification. This includes an email address, Slack channel, or workflow.

children.name: title
children.description: The title of the incident, which summarizes what happened.

children.name: relationships
children.description: The relationships the incident will have with other resources once created.

children.name: commander_user
children.description: Relationship to user.

children.name: data
children.description: Relationship to user object.

children.name: id
children.description: A unique identifier that represents the user.

children.name: type
children.description: Users resource type.

children.name: type
children.description: Incident resource type.

children.name: attributes

children.name: archived
children.description: Timestamp of when the incident was archived.

children.name: case_id
children.description: The incident case id.

children.name: created
children.description: Timestamp when the incident was created.

children.name: customer_impact_duration
children.description:
Length of the incident's customer impact in seconds.
Equals the difference between `customer_impact_start` and `customer_impact_end`.

children.name: customer_impact_end
children.description: Timestamp when customers were no longer impacted by the incident.

children.name: customer_impact_scope
children.description: A summary of the impact customers experienced during the incident.

children.name: customer_impact_start
children.description: Timestamp when customers began being impacted by the incident.

children.name: customer_impacted
children.description: A flag indicating whether the incident caused customer impact.

children.name: declared
children.description: Timestamp when the incident was declared.

children.name: declared_by
children.description: Incident's non Datadog creator.

children.name: image_48_px
children.description: Non Datadog creator `48px` image.

children.name: name
children.description: Non Datadog creator name.

children.name: declared_by_uuid
children.description: UUID of the user who declared the incident.

children.name: detected
children.description: Timestamp when the incident was detected.

children.name: fields
children.description: A condensed view of the user-defined fields attached to incidents.

children.name: incident_type_uuid
children.description: A unique identifier that represents an incident type.

children.name: is_test
children.description: A flag indicating whether the incident is a test incident.

children.name: modified
children.description: Timestamp when the incident was last modified.

children.name: non_datadog_creator
children.description: Incident's non Datadog creator.

children.name: image_48_px
children.description: Non Datadog creator `48px` image.

children.name: name
children.description: Non Datadog creator name.

children.name: notification_handles
children.description: Notification handles that will be notified of the incident during update.

children.name: display_name
children.description: The name of the notified handle.

children.name: handle
children.description: The handle used for the notification. This includes an email address, Slack channel, or workflow.

children.name: public_id
children.description: The monotonically increasing integer ID for the incident.

children.name: resolved
children.description: Timestamp when the incident's state was last changed from active or stable to resolved or completed.

children.name: severity
children.description: The incident severity.

children.name: state
children.description: The state incident.

children.name: time_to_detect
children.description:
The amount of time in seconds to detect the incident.
Equals the difference between `customer_impact_start` and `detected`.

children.name: time_to_internal_response
children.description: The amount of time in seconds to call incident after detection. Equals the difference of `detected` and `created`.

children.name: time_to_repair
children.description: The amount of time in seconds to resolve customer impact after detecting the issue. Equals the difference between `customer_impact_end` and `detected`.

children.name: time_to_resolve
children.description: The amount of time in seconds to resolve the incident after it was created. Equals the difference between `created` and `resolved`.

examples.name: Example

entries.code:
"""
Create an incident returns "CREATED" response
"""

from os import environ
from datadog_api_client import ApiClient, Configuration
from datadog_api_client.v2.api.incidents_api import IncidentsApi
from datadog_api_client.v2.model.incident_create_attributes import IncidentCreateAttributes
from datadog_api_client.v2.model.incident_create_data import IncidentCreateData
from datadog_api_client.v2.model.incident_create_relationships import IncidentCreateRelationships
from datadog_api_client.v2.model.incident_create_request import IncidentCreateRequest
from datadog_api_client.v2.model.incident_field_attributes_single_value import IncidentFieldAttributesSingleValue
from datadog_api_client.v2.model.incident_field_attributes_single_value_type import (
    IncidentFieldAttributesSingleValueType,
)
from datadog_api_client.v2.model.incident_type import IncidentType
from datadog_api_client.v2.model.nullable_relationship_to_user import NullableRelationshipToUser
from datadog_api_client.v2.model.nullable_relationship_to_user_data import NullableRelationshipToUserData
from datadog_api_client.v2.model.users_type import UsersType

# there is a valid "user" in the system
USER_DATA_ID = environ["USER_DATA_ID"]

body = IncidentCreateRequest(
    data=IncidentCreateData(
        type=IncidentType.INCIDENTS,
        attributes=IncidentCreateAttributes(
            title="Example-Incident",
            customer_impacted=False,
            fields=dict(
                state=IncidentFieldAttributesSingleValue(
                    type=IncidentFieldAttributesSingleValueType.DROPDOWN,
                    value="resolved",
                ),
            ),
        ),
        relationships=IncidentCreateRelationships(
            commander_user=NullableRelationshipToUser(
                data=NullableRelationshipToUserData(
                    type=UsersType.USERS,
                    id=USER_DATA_ID,
                ),
            ),
        ),
    ),
)

configuration = Configuration()
configuration.unstable_operations["create_incident"] = True
with ApiClient(configuration) as api_client:
    api_instance = IncidentsApi(api_client)
    response = api_instance.create_incident(body=body)

    print(response)
